1	"""
2	Process Theseus Codex uploads locally.
3	
4	Story
5	-----
6	The Codex web app (on Vercel) accepts file uploads into its Postgres
7	database but cannot run the Noosphere Python pipeline in that runtime —
8	Vercel's serverless Node has no Python interpreter. Uploads land as
9	``queued_offline`` with their ``textContent`` sitting in the DB until
10	someone runs this command on a machine that DOES have Noosphere.
11	
12	What it does
13	------------
14	Given an Upload id, this module:
15	
16	1. Connects to the Codex's Postgres (the shared Supabase instance — same
17	   DB, different tables than Noosphere would normally write to).
18	2. Reads the ``Upload`` row, pulls ``textContent``.
19	3. Extracts atomic claims from the text. Two extraction modes:
20	     - default / naive: rule-based sentence splitter that keeps only
21	       assertoric-looking sentences (filters questions, fragments, mere
22	       listings). Zero dependencies beyond the standard library, works
23	       without an LLM API key.
24	     - ``--with-llm``: delegates to the registered ``extract_claims``
25	       method (the Noosphere LLM pipeline). Higher quality, requires
26	       ``ANTHROPIC_API_KEY`` or ``OPENAI_API_KEY`` via noosphere.config.
27	4. Writes each extracted claim back into the Codex's ``Conclusion``
28	   table (tier=``open``, confidence=0.5) so it appears in the Codex web
29	   UI immediately.
30	5. Updates the Upload row to ``status='ingested'`` and sets
31	   ``claimsCount`` so the dashboard counters reflect the work.
32	6. Writes an ``AuditEvent`` row for traceability.
33	
34	Why write to the Codex schema directly instead of Noosphere's own
35	-----------------------------------------------------------------
36	The goal of this command is "user uploaded a file, user sees something
37	in the Codex UI after running Noosphere locally". Writing to Noosphere's
38	separate tables would require the Codex UI to also query those tables
39	(the existing Python-spawn bridges do this, but they don't work on
40	Vercel either). Writing directly to the Codex's ``Conclusion`` table
41	gives the end-to-end "I uploaded, I can see analysis" flow without any
42	additional infrastructure. Full Noosphere-side artifact/chunk/claim
43	storage can be added later via ``--also-write-noosphere``.
44	"""
45	
46	from __future__ import annotations
47	
48	import json
49	import os
50	import re
51	import sys
52	import uuid
53	from dataclasses import dataclass
54	from datetime import datetime, timezone
55	from typing import Optional
56	
57	try:
58	    import psycopg2
59	    import psycopg2.extras
60	except ImportError as e:  # pragma: no cover
61	    raise RuntimeError(
62	        "psycopg2 is required for the Codex bridge. "
63	        "Install with: pip install psycopg2-binary"
64	    ) from e
65	
66	
67	# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
68	# Postgres-safe sanitization.
69	#
70	# Postgres's UTF-8 enforcement rejects strings containing the NUL
71	# byte (``\u0000``) with "invalid byte sequence for encoding "UTF8":
72	# 0x00". PDF extraction, DOCX extraction, and even LLM output
73	# occasionally produce them. Every string that's about to be inserted
74	# into the Codex DB flows through ``_db_safe`` first so one bad
75	# byte doesn't nuke the whole ingest.
76	#
77	# We also strip other C0 control chars (except tab/newline/CR) and
78	# lone UTF-16 surrogate halves, which behave the same way on some
79	# server collations.
80	# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
81	
82	# C0 controls minus \t (\x09), \n (\x0a), \r (\x0d). Also nuke BOM.
83	_DB_UNSAFE = re.compile(r"[\x00-\x08\x0b\x0c\x0e-\x1f\ufeff]")
84	_DB_SURROGATE = re.compile(r"[\ud800-\udfff]")
85	
86	
87	def _db_safe(s: Optional[str], *, cap: Optional[int] = None) -> str:
88	    """Scrub for Postgres and optionally cap length.
89	
90	    ``None`` / non-string inputs return ``""`` so callers can unconditionally
91	    pass field values without null-checking first.
92	    """
93	    if s is None:
94	        return ""
95	    text = str(s)
96	    text = _DB_UNSAFE.sub("", text)
97	    text = _DB_SURROGATE.sub("", text)
98	    if cap is not None and len(text) > cap:
99	        text = text[:cap]
100	    return text
101	
102	
103	# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
104	# Conclusion dedup — normalised text form.
105	#
106	# The Codex DB has a `Conclusion.normalizedText` column populated by this
107	# helper and matched by a `(organizationId, normalizedText)` index. Any
108	# time this pipeline is about to write a new Conclusion we first look up
109	# an existing row with the same normalised key in the same org; if one
110	# exists we skip the INSERT and add this upload as an extra source via
111	# `ConclusionSource`. That's what makes deletion work correctly — a
112	# Conclusion whose one-and-only source is removed gets orphan-deleted,
113	# while a Conclusion supported by several uploads stays in place with
114	# the surviving uploads as its remaining sources.
115	#
116	# The formula (lower + strip + collapse whitespace) matches the SQL
117	# backfill in
118	# `prisma/migrations/20260420000000_conclusion_sources_and_derived_cascade`,
119	# so pre-existing rows and freshly-inserted rows share the same key.
120	# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
121	
122	_WS_COLLAPSE_RE = re.compile(r"\s+")
123	
124	
125	def _normalize_claim_text(text: str) -> str:
126	    """Return the dedup key for a claim. Empty input → empty key."""
127	    if not text:
128	        return ""
129	    return _WS_COLLAPSE_RE.sub(" ", text).strip().lower()
130	
131	
132	# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
133	# Naive claim extraction — works without an LLM. Conservative: keeps only
134	# sentences that look like first-person / declarative assertions, filters
135	# questions and fragments. Good enough for a first-pass Codex display.
136	# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
137	
138	
139	# Sentence-level tokens ending with a period / exclamation / line break.
140	# Non-greedy so we don't over-fuse sentences across paragraph breaks.
141	_SENT_RE = re.compile(r"[^.!?\n]+[.!?]+|[^.!?\n]+(?=\n|$)", re.MULTILINE)
142	
143	# Heuristic filters for sentences we SHOULDN'T promote to claims.
144	_QUESTION_RE = re.compile(r"\?\s*$")
145	_LIST_BULLET_RE = re.compile(r"^\s*[-*•\d]+[\.\)]?\s+")
146	_URL_RE = re.compile(r"https?://|www\.")
147	_HEADER_RE = re.compile(r"^#+\s|^={3,}\s*$|^-{3,}\s*$")
148	
149	
150	@dataclass
151	class NaiveClaim:
152	    text: str
153	    claim_type: str = "empirical"  # conservative default
154	
155	
156	def naive_extract_claims(text: str, *, max_claims: int = 40) -> list[NaiveClaim]:
157	    """
158	    Sentence-split a blob of text and keep only sentences that look like
159	    substantive claims:
160	      * length ≥ 40 chars (filters "Yes.", "Ok.", lone bullets)
161	      * length ≤ 400 chars (filters run-on paragraphs that aren't atomic)
162	      * not a question (``?`` at end)
163	      * not a list bullet / markdown header
164	      * contains at least one verb-like word (``is|are|was|were|should|must|can|may|will``)
165	        OR a contextual connector (``because|therefore|given|since``)
166	
167	    Returns at most ``max_claims`` so a 50-page transcript doesn't blow
168	    up the Codex with thousands of rows.
169	    """
170	    out: list[NaiveClaim] = []
171	    cleaned = _HEADER_RE.sub("", text)
172	    # Normalise whitespace so sentence splitting doesn't fragment on line
173	    # wraps inside a paragraph. We preserve paragraph breaks (double-newline)
174	    # because they are a useful signal for separating assertions; we just
175	    # rejoin line wraps WITHIN a paragraph into spaces.
176	    cleaned = re.sub(r"(?<!\n)\n(?!\n)", " ", cleaned)
177	    cleaned = re.sub(r"[ \t]+", " ", cleaned)
178	    # Walk sentences.
179	    for match in _SENT_RE.finditer(cleaned):
180	        raw = match.group(0).strip()
181	        if not raw:
182	            continue
183	        if len(raw) < 40 or len(raw) > 400:
184	            continue
185	        if _QUESTION_RE.search(raw):
186	            continue
187	        if _LIST_BULLET_RE.match(raw):
188	            # Strip the bullet and re-check length.
189	            stripped = _LIST_BULLET_RE.sub("", raw).strip()
190	            if len(stripped) < 40 or len(stripped) > 400:
191	                continue
192	            raw = stripped
193	        if _URL_RE.search(raw):
194	            # Drop sentences that are mostly URLs — they're rarely claims.
195	            continue
196	        lower = raw.lower()
197	        has_verb = bool(
198	            re.search(
199	                r"\b(is|are|was|were|should|must|can|may|will|would|do|does|did|have|has|had|seems|appears|means|implies|suggests|shows|proves|causes|requires|matters)\b",
200	                lower,
201	            )
202	        )
203	        has_connector = bool(
204	            re.search(r"\b(because|therefore|thus|hence|since|given that)\b", lower)
205	        )
206	        if not (has_verb or has_connector):
207	            continue
208	        # Classify: rough taxonomy based on vocabulary.
209	        claim_type = "empirical"
210	        if re.search(r"\b(should|must|ought|right|wrong|better|worse|good|bad)\b", lower):
211	            claim_type = "normative"
212	        elif re.search(
213	            r"\b(method|framework|approach|process|procedure|methodology)\b", lower
214	        ):
215	            claim_type = "methodological"
216	        elif re.search(r"\b(will|won't|shall|going to|expect|predict)\b", lower):
217	            claim_type = "predictive"
218	        out.append(NaiveClaim(text=raw, claim_type=claim_type))
219	        if len(out) >= max_claims:
220	            break
221	    return out
222	
223	
224	# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
225	# LLM-backed extraction — uses Noosphere's registered ``extract_claims`` method.
226	# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
227	
228	
229	def llm_extract_claims(
230	    text: str,
231	    *,
232	    chunk_char_size: int = 3500,
233	    max_chunks: int = 10,
234	    upload_id: str = "upload",
235	) -> list[NaiveClaim]:
236	    """LLM-backed extraction via Noosphere's ``extract_claims`` method.
237	
238	    Raises if the LLM is unconfigured — the caller is expected to have
239	    passed --with-llm deliberately.
240	    """
241	    from noosphere.methods.extract_claims import (
242	        ExtractClaimsInput,
243	        extract_claims,
244	    )
245	
246	    # Chunk the text so no single LLM call blows past the context budget.
247	    # Paragraph splits preferred; fall back to hard-cut if a paragraph is
248	    # itself huge.
249	    raw_paras = re.split(r"\n\s*\n+", text)
250	    chunks: list[str] = []
251	    buf: list[str] = []
252	    size = 0
253	    for p in raw_paras:
254	        if size + len(p) > chunk_char_size and buf:
255	            chunks.append("\n\n".join(buf))
256	            buf = []
257	            size = 0
258	        if len(p) > chunk_char_size:
259	            # Hard-split the monster paragraph.
260	            for i in range(0, len(p), chunk_char_size):
261	                chunks.append(p[i : i + chunk_char_size])
262	        else:
263	            buf.append(p)
264	            size += len(p) + 2
265	    if buf:
266	        chunks.append("\n\n".join(buf))
267	
268	    chunks = chunks[:max_chunks]
269	
270	    out: list[NaiveClaim] = []
271	    for i, chunk in enumerate(chunks):
272	        result = extract_claims(
273	            ExtractClaimsInput(
274	                chunk_text=chunk,
275	                chunk_id=f"{upload_id}_chunk_{i}",
276	                episode_id=upload_id,
277	                speaker_name="founder",
278	                speaker_role="founder",
279	            )
280	        )
281	        for c in result.claims:
282	            if not c.text.strip():
283	                continue
284	            # Drop claims the LLM flagged as external (interview prompts,
285	            # counter-positions, quoted views) so they never land in the
286	            # founder's Conclusion canon. This is defense-in-depth: the
287	            # extractor already labels them, the bridge refuses to
288	            # propagate them.
289	            if getattr(c, "is_author_assertion", True) is False:
290	                continue
291	            out.append(NaiveClaim(text=c.text.strip(), claim_type=c.claim_type))
292	    return out
293	
294	
295	# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
296	# Core pipeline — connects to Codex Postgres, reads upload, writes results.
297	# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
298	
299	
300	@dataclass
301	class IngestFromCodexResult:
302	    upload_id: str
303	    title: str
304	    num_claims_extracted: int
305	    num_conclusions_written: int
306	    num_contradictions_written: int
307	    num_open_questions_written: int
308	    num_research_suggestions_written: int
309	    dry_run: bool
310	    mode: str  # "naive" | "llm"
311	
312	
313	# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
314	# Contradiction detection — naive (always runs) + LLM-augmented (optional).
315	# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
316	
317	
318	@dataclass
319	class DetectedContradiction:
320	    """A contradicting claim pair. `a_idx` / `b_idx` refer to indices in
321	    the claim list we just extracted; if one side is an existing firm
322	    Conclusion (cross-upload contradiction) we use `b_existing_id` instead
323	    of `b_idx`."""
324	    a_idx: int
325	    b_idx: int | None  # None when b is an existing firm Conclusion
326	    b_existing_id: str | None  # Conclusion.id when b is from the firm corpus
327	    severity: float   # 0..1
328	    narrative: str
329	    source: str       # "heuristic" | "llm"
330	
331	
332	_NEGATION_TOKENS = re.compile(
333	    r"\b(not|never|no|none|nothing|nobody|nowhere|n'?t|cannot|can'?t|won'?t|"
334	    r"shouldn'?t|wouldn'?t|couldn'?t|isn'?t|aren'?t|wasn'?t|weren'?t|"
335	    r"doesn'?t|didn'?t|don'?t|haven'?t|hasn'?t|hadn'?t)\b",
336	    flags=re.IGNORECASE,
337	)
338	_STOPWORDS = {
339	    "the", "a", "an", "is", "are", "was", "were", "be", "been", "being",
340	    "and", "or", "but", "if", "then", "of", "in", "on", "at", "to", "for",
341	    "by", "with", "from", "as", "that", "this", "these", "those", "it",
342	    "its", "we", "our", "you", "they", "them", "their", "i", "me", "my",
343	    "he", "she", "his", "her", "has", "have", "had", "do", "does", "did",
344	    "will", "would", "should", "could", "can", "may", "must", "ought",
345	    "so", "than", "such", "some", "any", "all", "every", "each", "both",
346	    "more", "most", "much", "many", "few",
347	}
348	
349	
350	def _content_tokens(text: str) -> set[str]:
351	    toks = re.findall(r"\b[a-z][a-z']+\b", text.lower())
352	    return {t for t in toks if t not in _STOPWORDS and len(t) >= 4}
353	
354	
355	def _has_negation(text: str) -> bool:
356	    return bool(_NEGATION_TOKENS.search(text))
357	
358	
359	def naive_detect_contradictions(
360	    claims: list[NaiveClaim],
361	) -> list[DetectedContradiction]:
362	    """
363	    Heuristic pairwise contradiction scan on the new claims. Flags pairs
364	    where:
365	      (1) their content vocabulary overlaps significantly (same subject), AND
366	      (2) exactly one contains a negation marker (opposite polarity).
367	
368	    This catches the obvious "X should always …" vs "X should not …" case
369	    without needing an NLI model. Recall is modest; precision is decent
370	    enough that the flagged pairs are usually worth a human's attention.
371	    """
372	    out: list[DetectedContradiction] = []
373	    tok_sets = [_content_tokens(c.text) for c in claims]
374	    neg_flags = [_has_negation(c.text) for c in claims]
375	    for i in range(len(claims)):
376	        for j in range(i + 1, len(claims)):
377	            if neg_flags[i] == neg_flags[j]:
378	                continue  # both affirmative or both negative — no polarity flip
379	            if not tok_sets[i] or not tok_sets[j]:
380	                continue
381	            shared = tok_sets[i] & tok_sets[j]
382	            # Require meaningful overlap — otherwise it's just two unrelated
383	            # sentences that happen to have different polarity. A Jaccard
384	            # of 0.35 on content tokens is a useful middle ground.
385	            smaller = min(len(tok_sets[i]), len(tok_sets[j]))
386	            if smaller == 0 or (len(shared) / smaller) < 0.35:
387	                continue
388	            # Severity proxy: how much do they overlap? More overlap = more
389	            # directly contradictory.
390	            overlap_ratio = len(shared) / smaller
391	            severity = min(0.9, 0.4 + overlap_ratio * 0.5)
392	            out.append(
393	                DetectedContradiction(
394	                    a_idx=i,
395	                    b_idx=j,
396	                    b_existing_id=None,
397	                    severity=severity,
398	                    narrative=(
399	                        f"Heuristic: shared vocabulary {sorted(shared)[:6]} "
400	                        f"but opposite polarity."
401	                    ),
402	                    source="heuristic",
403	                )
404	            )
405	    return out
406	
407	
408	def llm_detect_contradictions_and_questions(
409	    claims: list[NaiveClaim],
410	    firm_conclusions: list[tuple[str, str]],  # (id, text) of existing firm claims
411	    *,
412	    upload_title: str,
413	) -> dict:
414	    """
415	    Single LLM call that does all three analysis tasks at once —
416	    contradictions, open questions, research suggestions. Putting them in
417	    one prompt is:
418	      (a) cheaper (single context load);
419	      (b) more coherent — the questions are generated in light of the
420	          contradictions the LLM just identified, instead of being
421	          independently invented.
422	
423	    Returns a dict shaped like:
424	      {
425	        "contradictions": [{"a": int, "b": int, "severity": float, "narrative": str}],
426	        "cross_contradictions": [{"a": int, "existing_id": str, "severity": float, "narrative": str}],
427	        "open_questions": [{"summary": str, "unresolved_reason": str, "a": int, "b": int|None}],
428	        "research_suggestions": [{"title": str, "summary": str, "rationale": str}],
429	      }
430	    Missing keys are backfilled to empty lists so the caller never
431	    explodes on a partial response.
432	
433	    Raises on LLM failure so the caller can decide whether to fall back
434	    to heuristic-only mode.
435	    """
436	    from noosphere.llm import llm_client_from_settings
437	
438	    llm = llm_client_from_settings()
439	
440	    new_lines = "\n".join(
441	        f"  [{i}] {c.text}" for i, c in enumerate(claims)
442	    )
443	    firm_lines = (
444	        "\n".join(f"  <{fid[:10]}> {txt[:240]}" for fid, txt in firm_conclusions[:40])
445	        if firm_conclusions
446	        else "  (none yet — this is the first upload for this firm.)"
447	    )
448	
449	    system = (
450	        "You are an analyst helping a firm surface contradictions, open "
451	        "questions, and research directions from a newly uploaded document. "
452	        "Answer ONLY in JSON matching the schema below. Be conservative — "
453	        "only flag genuine contradictions, not mere topical overlap. Only "
454	        "propose questions a founder could actually research. Never "
455	        "fabricate evidence."
456	    )
457	    user = f"""New document: "{upload_title}"
458	
459	CLAIMS extracted from this document (indices in brackets):
460	{new_lines}
461	
462	FIRM'S EXISTING CONCLUSIONS (for cross-upload contradiction detection):
463	{firm_lines}
464	
465	Return a single JSON object with these keys:
466	{{
467	  "contradictions": [
468	    {{"a": <idx>, "b": <idx>, "severity": <0..1>, "narrative": "<≤200 chars, why they conflict>"}}
469	  ],
470	  "cross_contradictions": [
471	    {{"a": <idx>, "existing_id": "<existing firm claim id prefix>", "severity": <0..1>, "narrative": "<≤200 chars>"}}
472	  ],
473	  "open_questions": [
474	    {{"summary": "<≤180 chars, a concrete question>", "unresolved_reason": "<why unresolved>", "a": <idx or null>, "b": <idx or null>}}
475	  ],
476	  "research_suggestions": [
477	    {{"title": "<short title>", "summary": "<one sentence>", "rationale": "<why this matters>"}}
478	  ]
479	}}
480	
481	Rules:
482	- 0-5 contradictions, 0-3 cross_contradictions, 3-6 open_questions, 2-4 research_suggestions.
483	- If no real contradictions exist, return [] for that list. Do not pad.
484	- `a` and `b` must be valid indices into the CLAIMS list above.
485	- Output ONLY the JSON object, no prose."""
486	
487	    raw = llm.complete(system=system, user=user, max_tokens=2500)
488	    m = re.search(r"\{.*\}", raw, re.DOTALL)
489	    if not m:
490	        raise RuntimeError(f"LLM returned non-JSON: {raw[:200]}")
491	    payload = json.loads(m.group(0))
492	    return {
493	        "contradictions": payload.get("contradictions", []) or [],
494	        "cross_contradictions": payload.get("cross_contradictions", []) or [],
495	        "open_questions": payload.get("open_questions", []) or [],
496	        "research_suggestions": payload.get("research_suggestions", []) or [],
497	    }
498	
499	
500	def _resolve_codex_db_url(explicit: Optional[str]) -> str:
501	    if explicit:
502	        return explicit
503	    for env in (
504	        "THESEUS_CODEX_DATABASE_URL",
505	        "CODEX_DATABASE_URL",
506	        "DIRECT_URL",
507	        "DATABASE_URL",
508	    ):
509	        val = os.environ.get(env, "").strip()
510	        if val:
511	            return val
512	    raise RuntimeError(
513	        "No Codex Postgres URL found. Pass --codex-db-url, or export "
514	        "THESEUS_CODEX_DATABASE_URL / CODEX_DATABASE_URL / DIRECT_URL / "
515	        "DATABASE_URL. The Supabase DIRECT_URL (port 5432, not the 6543 "
516	        "pooler) is preferred — the pooler can strip advisory locks some "
517	        "ORMs rely on."
518	    )
519	
520	
521	def _short_cuid_like() -> str:
522	    """Simple unique id for Conclusion / AuditEvent rows. Prisma normally
523	    uses cuid() but the DB only enforces uniqueness, so a uuid4-derived
524	    id works fine and keeps this file Python-only (no cuid dep)."""
525	    return "c_" + uuid.uuid4().hex[:24]
526	
527	
528	def ingest_from_codex(
529	    upload_id: str,
530	    *,
531	    codex_db_url: Optional[str] = None,
532	    use_llm: bool = False,
533	    max_claims: int = 40,
534	    dry_run: bool = False,
535	    organization_slug_filter: Optional[str] = None,
536	) -> IngestFromCodexResult:
537	    """
538	    Read a Codex Upload, extract claims, write Conclusions back into the
539	    same DB. Returns a small result object so the CLI can print a summary.
540	    """
541	    url = _resolve_codex_db_url(codex_db_url)
542	    conn = psycopg2.connect(url)
543	    try:
544	        cur = conn.cursor(cursor_factory=psycopg2.extras.RealDictCursor)
545	
546	        # ── Fetch the upload ─────────────────────────────────────────────
547	        cur.execute(
548	            '''SELECT id, "organizationId", "founderId", title, "textContent",
549	                      status, "mimeType", "originalName", "filePath", "fileSize"
550	               FROM "Upload" WHERE id = %s''',
551	            (upload_id,),
552	        )
553	        row = cur.fetchone()
554	        if row is None:
555	            raise RuntimeError(f"Upload {upload_id} not found in the Codex DB.")
556	
557	        # ── Fetch-and-extract via the MIME dispatcher ───────────────────
558	        # ``fetch_upload_content`` returns TextContent when the DB has
559	        # textContent populated, or BinaryContent (bytes + mime) when the
560	        # row points at Supabase Storage / a local path. ``dispatch``
561	        # then routes to the appropriate extractor (passthrough for text,
562	        # Whisper for audio — see prompt 02, pypdf for PDF — see prompt
563	        # 03). Failures are marked on the Upload row with a structured
564	        # reason so the dashboard (prompt 04) can surface them.
565	        from noosphere.extractors import (
566	            ExtractionFailed,
567	            UnsupportedMimeType,
568	            dispatch,
569	        )
570	        from noosphere.extractors._content_fetcher import fetch_upload_content
571	
572	        # Mark `extracting` as we enter the MIME dispatch. This is the
573	        # stage where Whisper/pypdf run; the Codex dashboard shows an
574	        # amber pulsing badge so the founder can see the pipeline is
575	        # alive before any LLM calls happen.
576	        if not dry_run:
577	            cur.execute(
578	                'UPDATE "Upload" SET status = %s WHERE id = %s',
579	                ("extracting", upload_id),
580	            )
581	            conn.commit()
582	
583	        try:
584	            content = fetch_upload_content(row, conn)
585	            extracted = dispatch(content)
586	        except (ExtractionFailed, UnsupportedMimeType) as exc:
587	            reason = (
588	                f"unsupported_mime: {exc}"
589	                if isinstance(exc, UnsupportedMimeType)
590	                else f"extraction_failed: {exc}"
591	            )
592	            if not dry_run:
593	                cur.execute(
594	                    '''UPDATE "Upload"
595	                       SET status = %s, "errorMessage" = %s
596	                       WHERE id = %s''',
597	                    ("failed", _db_safe(reason, cap=400), upload_id),
598	                )
599	                conn.commit()
600	            raise
601	
602	        text = extracted.text
603	        print(
604	            f"extraction method={extracted.extraction_method} "
605	            f"source_format={extracted.source_format} "
606	            f"warnings={len(extracted.warnings)}"
607	        )
608	        for w in extracted.warnings:
609	            print(f"  warning: {w}")
610	
611	        if not text or not text.strip():
612	            raise RuntimeError(
613	                f"Upload {upload_id}: extractor {extracted.extraction_method!r} "
614	                "returned empty text."
615	            )
616	
617	        # Optional sanity check on tenant if the caller passed a slug — protects
618	        # against running this against the wrong Codex tenant by mistake.
619	        if organization_slug_filter:
620	            cur.execute(
621	                'SELECT slug FROM "Organization" WHERE id = %s',
622	                (row["organizationId"],),
623	            )
624	            org = cur.fetchone()
625	            if not org or org["slug"] != organization_slug_filter:
626	                raise RuntimeError(
627	                    f"Upload {upload_id} belongs to org {org['slug'] if org else '?'}, "
628	                    f"not the expected slug '{organization_slug_filter}'."
629	                )
630	
631	        # ── Intermediate beat: text is ready, claim extraction next ──────
632	        # The dashboard shows `awaiting_ingest` momentarily between the
633	        # two heavy stages. For uploads with no PromptSeparator/LLM work
634	        # this flashes by — the point is that if the pipeline stalls,
635	        # the stall point is visible.
636	        if not dry_run:
637	            cur.execute(
638	                'UPDATE "Upload" SET status = %s WHERE id = %s',
639	                ("awaiting_ingest", upload_id),
640	            )
641	            conn.commit()
642	
643	        # ── Mark processing (non-dry only) ───────────────────────────────
644	        if not dry_run:
645	            cur.execute(
646	                'UPDATE "Upload" SET status = %s WHERE id = %s',
647	                ("processing", upload_id),
648	            )
649	            conn.commit()
650	
651	        # ── Strip interview prompts / external quotes BEFORE extraction ─
652	        # Defense-in-depth against misattributing external text as
653	        # founder claims. Failures here log & fall through to raw text.
654	        try:
655	            from noosphere.mitigations.prompt_separator import PromptSeparator
656	            separated = PromptSeparator().separate(text, source_type="written")
657	            print(
658	                f"prompt_separator founder_sections={len(separated.founder_sections)} "
659	                f"prompt_sections={len(separated.prompt_sections)} "
660	                f"confidence={separated.confidence:.2f}"
661	            )
662	            if separated.founder_sections:
663	                text = separated.founder_text
664	        except Exception as sep_err:
665	            print(f"prompt_separator_failed: {sep_err}")
666	
667	        # ── Extract claims ───────────────────────────────────────────────
668	        if use_llm:
669	            claims = llm_extract_claims(text, upload_id=upload_id, max_chunks=10)
670	            mode = "llm"
671	        else:
672	            claims = naive_extract_claims(text, max_claims=max_claims)
673	            mode = "naive"
674	
675	        if dry_run:
676	            return IngestFromCodexResult(
677	                upload_id=upload_id,
678	                title=row["title"] or row["originalName"] or upload_id,
679	                num_claims_extracted=len(claims),
680	                num_conclusions_written=0,
681	                num_contradictions_written=0,
682	                num_open_questions_written=0,
683	                num_research_suggestions_written=0,
684	                dry_run=True,
685	                mode=mode,
686	            )
687	
688	        # ── Insert conclusions (with dedup + source linking) ─────────────
689	        # We also track a parallel list of (claim_index → conclusion_id)
690	        # so we can cite the right Conclusion rows from contradictions
691	        # and open questions below. Claims that are too short to insert
692	        # won't appear in this map (their index maps to None).
693	        #
694	        # Dedup pass: before inserting a Conclusion we look for an
695	        # existing row in this org with the same normalised text. If one
696	        # exists, we DON'T duplicate — we just add this upload as an
697	        # additional source via ConclusionSource. That's the mechanic
698	        # that lets a Conclusion survive when one of its two sources is
699	        # later deleted. A fresh insert writes both the Conclusion AND a
700	        # ConclusionSource row.
701	        now = datetime.now(timezone.utc)
702	        written = 0  # NEW conclusions inserted
703	        deduped = 0  # claims that matched an existing conclusion
704	        claim_idx_to_conclusion_id: list[str | None] = [None] * len(claims)
705	        # Pre-compute a sanitized source label we'll reuse in every row's
706	        # rationale/sessionLabel. Cap at 300 so a giant filename can't blow
707	        # the text column.
708	        source_label = _db_safe(
709	            row["title"] or row["originalName"] or upload_id,
710	            cap=300,
711	        )
712	        for idx, claim in enumerate(claims):
713	            claim_text = _db_safe(claim.text, cap=4_000).strip()
714	            if len(claim_text) < 20:
715	                continue
716	            normalized = _normalize_claim_text(claim_text)
717	            if not normalized:
718	                continue
719	
720	            # Dedup lookup — matching is scoped to the same org.
721	            cur.execute(
722	                '''SELECT id FROM "Conclusion"
723	                   WHERE "organizationId" = %s AND "normalizedText" = %s
724	                   LIMIT 1''',
725	                (row["organizationId"], normalized),
726	            )
727	            existing = cur.fetchone()
728	
729	            if existing:
730	                cid = existing["id"]
731	                # Attach this upload as another source. ON CONFLICT is
732	                # a defensive no-op in case the user is re-running the
733	                # ingest for the same upload.
734	                cur.execute(
735	                    '''INSERT INTO "ConclusionSource"
736	                       ("conclusionId", "uploadId", "createdAt")
737	                       VALUES (%s, %s, %s)
738	                       ON CONFLICT ("conclusionId", "uploadId") DO NOTHING''',
739	                    (cid, upload_id, now),
740	                )
741	                claim_idx_to_conclusion_id[idx] = cid
742	                deduped += 1
743	                continue
744	
745	            cid = _short_cuid_like()
746	            noosphere_id = f"ing_{upload_id}_{idx}"
747	            cur.execute(
748	                '''INSERT INTO "Conclusion"
749	                   (id, "organizationId", "noosphereId", text, "normalizedText",
750	                    "confidenceTier", rationale, "supportingPrincipleIds",
751	                    "evidenceChainClaimIds", "dissentClaimIds",
752	                    confidence, "topicHint", "createdAt")
753	                   VALUES (%s, %s, %s, %s, %s, %s, %s, %s, %s, %s, %s, %s, %s)
754	                   ON CONFLICT ("noosphereId") DO NOTHING''',
755	                (
756	                    cid,
757	                    row["organizationId"],
758	                    noosphere_id,
759	                    claim_text,
760	                    normalized,
761	                    "open",
762	                    _db_safe(f"Extracted from upload: {source_label}", cap=500),
763	                    json.dumps([]),
764	                    json.dumps([]),
765	                    json.dumps([]),
766	                    0.5,
767	                    _db_safe(claim.claim_type, cap=64),
768	                    now,
769	                ),
770	            )
771	            if cur.rowcount == 1:
772	                written += 1
773	                claim_idx_to_conclusion_id[idx] = cid
774	                cur.execute(
775	                    '''INSERT INTO "ConclusionSource"
776	                       ("conclusionId", "uploadId", "createdAt")
777	                       VALUES (%s, %s, %s)
778	                       ON CONFLICT ("conclusionId", "uploadId") DO NOTHING''',
779	                    (cid, upload_id, now),
780	                )
781	
782	        # ── Contradiction detection ─────────────────────────────────────
783	        # Always run the heuristic pass (cheap, no deps). If LLM is
784	        # available AND use_llm=True, do the combined contradictions +
785	        # questions + research pass for higher quality.
786	        contradictions_written = 0
787	        open_questions_written = 0
788	        research_suggestions_written = 0
789	
790	        heuristic_pairs = naive_detect_contradictions(claims)
791	
792	        llm_payload: dict | None = None
793	        llm_error: str | None = None
794	        if use_llm:
795	            try:
796	                # Pull up to 40 recent firm conclusions for cross-upload checking.
797	                cur.execute(
798	                    '''SELECT id, text FROM "Conclusion"
799	                       WHERE "organizationId" = %s
800	                         AND "noosphereId" NOT LIKE %s
801	                       ORDER BY "createdAt" DESC LIMIT 40''',
802	                    (row["organizationId"], f"ing_{upload_id}_%"),
803	                )
804	                firm_conclusions = [(r["id"], r["text"]) for r in cur.fetchall()]
805	                llm_payload = llm_detect_contradictions_and_questions(
806	                    claims,
807	                    firm_conclusions,
808	                    upload_title=row["title"] or row["originalName"] or upload_id,
809	                )
810	            except Exception as exc:
811	                # LLM failure is non-fatal — we still have the heuristic
812	                # contradictions and the inserted Conclusions. Record the
813	                # reason so the caller can surface it in the process log.
814	                llm_error = f"{type(exc).__name__}: {exc}"
815	
816	        # ── Write contradictions (both sources merged) ──────────────────
817	        # Every contradiction carries `sourceUploadId = upload_id` — it's
818	        # the product of THIS ingestion run. Cross-upload contradictions
819	        # (where claim B is an existing firm Conclusion from a DIFFERENT
820	        # upload) also get the current upload as their source, since that
821	        # upload is the one that surfaced the conflict. Deleting either
822	        # upload will remove the contradiction — the current upload via
823	        # its `sourceUploadId`, the other upload via claimBId being
824	        # orphan-deleted when ITS source Conclusion loses all sources.
825	        for pair in heuristic_pairs:
826	            a_cid = claim_idx_to_conclusion_id[pair.a_idx]
827	            b_cid = (
828	                claim_idx_to_conclusion_id[pair.b_idx]
829	                if pair.b_idx is not None
830	                else pair.b_existing_id
831	            )
832	            if not a_cid or not b_cid:
833	                continue
834	            cur.execute(
835	                '''INSERT INTO "Contradiction"
836	                   (id, "organizationId", "claimAId", "claimBId", severity,
837	                    "sixLayerJson", narrative, "sourceUploadId", "createdAt")
838	                   VALUES (%s, %s, %s, %s, %s, %s, %s, %s, %s)''',
839	                (
840	                    "x_" + uuid.uuid4().hex[:24],
841	                    row["organizationId"],
842	                    a_cid,
843	                    b_cid,
844	                    float(pair.severity),
845	                    json.dumps({"source": "heuristic"}),
846	                    _db_safe(pair.narrative, cap=2_000),
847	                    upload_id,
848	                    now,
849	                ),
850	            )
851	            contradictions_written += cur.rowcount
852	
853	        # LLM contradictions (within-upload).
854	        if llm_payload:
855	            for item in llm_payload.get("contradictions", []):
856	                try:
857	                    a = int(item["a"])
858	                    b = int(item["b"])
859	                    sev = float(item.get("severity", 0.6))
860	                    narrative = str(item.get("narrative", ""))[:500]
861	                except (KeyError, ValueError, TypeError):
862	                    continue
863	                a_cid = claim_idx_to_conclusion_id[a] if 0 <= a < len(claims) else None
864	                b_cid = claim_idx_to_conclusion_id[b] if 0 <= b < len(claims) else None
865	                if not a_cid or not b_cid or a_cid == b_cid:
866	                    continue
867	                cur.execute(
868	                    '''INSERT INTO "Contradiction"
869	                       (id, "organizationId", "claimAId", "claimBId", severity,
870	                        "sixLayerJson", narrative, "sourceUploadId", "createdAt")
871	                       VALUES (%s, %s, %s, %s, %s, %s, %s, %s, %s)''',
872	                    (
873	                        "x_" + uuid.uuid4().hex[:24],
874	                        row["organizationId"],
875	                        a_cid,
876	                        b_cid,
877	                        sev,
878	                        json.dumps({"source": "llm"}),
879	                        _db_safe(narrative or "LLM flagged as contradictory", cap=2_000),
880	                        upload_id,
881	                        now,
882	                    ),
883	                )
884	                contradictions_written += cur.rowcount
885	
886	            # Cross-upload contradictions (new claim vs existing firm claim).
887	            # The LLM returns a prefix like "abc1234…"; we match by prefix
888	            # against firm_conclusions' full ids.
889	            firm_id_lookup = {fid[:10]: fid for fid, _ in firm_conclusions}
890	            for item in llm_payload.get("cross_contradictions", []):
891	                try:
892	                    a = int(item["a"])
893	                    existing_prefix = str(item.get("existing_id", ""))[:10]
894	                    sev = float(item.get("severity", 0.6))
895	                    narrative = str(item.get("narrative", ""))[:500]
896	                except (KeyError, ValueError, TypeError):
897	                    continue
898	                a_cid = claim_idx_to_conclusion_id[a] if 0 <= a < len(claims) else None
899	                existing_id = firm_id_lookup.get(existing_prefix)
900	                if not a_cid or not existing_id or a_cid == existing_id:
901	                    continue
902	                cur.execute(
903	                    '''INSERT INTO "Contradiction"
904	                       (id, "organizationId", "claimAId", "claimBId", severity,
905	                        "sixLayerJson", narrative, "sourceUploadId", "createdAt")
906	                       VALUES (%s, %s, %s, %s, %s, %s, %s, %s, %s)''',
907	                    (
908	                        "x_" + uuid.uuid4().hex[:24],
909	                        row["organizationId"],
910	                        a_cid,
911	                        existing_id,
912	                        sev,
913	                        json.dumps({"source": "llm", "cross_upload": True}),
914	                        _db_safe(narrative or "LLM cross-upload contradiction", cap=2_000),
915	                        upload_id,
916	                        now,
917	                    ),
918	                )
919	                contradictions_written += cur.rowcount
920	
921	        # ── Write open questions (LLM only) ─────────────────────────────
922	        if llm_payload:
923	            for item in llm_payload.get("open_questions", []):
924	                try:
925	                    summary = str(item.get("summary", "")).strip()[:500]
926	                    reason = str(item.get("unresolved_reason", "")).strip()[:500]
927	                except (KeyError, TypeError):
928	                    continue
929	                if not summary:
930	                    continue
931	                a = item.get("a")
932	                b = item.get("b")
933	                a_cid = (
934	                    claim_idx_to_conclusion_id[int(a)]
935	                    if isinstance(a, int) and 0 <= a < len(claims)
936	                    else None
937	                )
938	                b_cid = (
939	                    claim_idx_to_conclusion_id[int(b)]
940	                    if isinstance(b, int) and 0 <= b < len(claims)
941	                    else None
942	                )
943	                # OpenQuestion requires claimAId + claimBId NOT NULL; if the
944	                # LLM didn't cite any, anchor both to the first inserted
945	                # conclusion as a minimal placeholder so the row is valid.
946	                # A future schema migration could make these nullable.
947	                fallback = next(
948	                    (cid for cid in claim_idx_to_conclusion_id if cid is not None),
949	                    None,
950	                )
951	                a_cid = a_cid or fallback
952	                b_cid = b_cid or fallback
953	                if not a_cid or not b_cid:

957	                       (id, "organizationId", "noosphereId", summary,

959	                        "layerDisagreementSummary", "sourceUploadId",

961	                       VALUES (%s, %s, %s, %s, %s, %s, %s, %s, %s, %s)''',
962	                    (

966	                        _db_safe(summary, cap=2_000),
967	                        a_cid,

976	
977	        # ── Write research suggestions (LLM only) ───────────────────────
978	        if llm_payload:
979	            for item in llm_payload.get("research_suggestions", []):
980	                try:
981	                    title = str(item.get("title", "")).strip()[:300]
982	                    summary = str(item.get("summary", "")).strip()[:1000]
983	                    rationale = str(item.get("rationale", "")).strip()[:1000]
984	                except (KeyError, TypeError):
985	                    continue
986	                if not title or not summary:
987	                    continue
988	                cur.execute(
989	                    '''INSERT INTO "ResearchSuggestion"
990	                       (id, "organizationId", "noosphereId", title, summary,
991	                        rationale, "readingUris", "sessionLabel",
992	                        "suggestedForFounderId", "sourceUploadId", "createdAt")
993	                       VALUES (%s, %s, %s, %s, %s, %s, %s, %s, %s, %s, %s)''',
994	                    (
995	                        "rs_" + uuid.uuid4().hex[:24],
996	                        row["organizationId"],
997	                        f"rs_{upload_id}_{research_suggestions_written}",
998	                        _db_safe(title, cap=300),
999	                        _db_safe(summary, cap=2_000),
1000	                        _db_safe(rationale or "LLM-generated during ingest", cap=2_000),
1001	                        json.dumps([]),
1002	                        source_label,
1003	                        row["founderId"],
1004	                        upload_id,
1005	                        now,
1006	                    ),
1007	                )
1008	                research_suggestions_written += cur.rowcount
1009	
1010	        # ── Update upload row ────────────────────────────────────────────
1011	        # claimsCount reflects BOTH newly-inserted conclusions AND ones
1012	        # this upload re-sourced (via dedup) — the number of distinct
1013	        # claims this upload anchors, not just the fresh inserts. That
1014	        # matches the user-facing "N claims extracted" semantics better
1015	        # than a raw INSERT count would.
1016	        # `extractionMethod` is the caption the Codex dashboard shows
1017	        # under an ingested row ("transcribed locally (faster-whisper)",
1018	        # "extracted with pypdf"…). Written on the same UPDATE as the
1019	        # terminal status so a partial success leaves a consistent row.
1020	        cur.execute(
1021	            '''UPDATE "Upload"
1022	               SET status = %s,
1023	                   "claimsCount" = %s,
1024	                   "errorMessage" = NULL,
1025	                   "extractionMethod" = %s
1026	               WHERE id = %s''',
1027	            (
1028	                "ingested",
1029	                written + deduped,
1030	                _db_safe(extracted.extraction_method, cap=64),
1031	                upload_id,
1032	            ),
1033	        )
1034	
1035	        # ── Audit event ──────────────────────────────────────────────────
1036	        audit_detail = (
1037	            f"Noosphere {mode} ingest · "
1038	            f"{written} new conclusions · "
1039	            f"{deduped} re-sourced · "
1040	            f"{contradictions_written} contradictions · "
1041	            f"{open_questions_written} open questions · "
1042	            f"{research_suggestions_written} research suggestions"
1043	        )
1044	        if llm_error:
1045	            audit_detail += f" · LLM pass failed: {llm_error[:80]}"
1046	        cur.execute(
1047	            '''INSERT INTO "AuditEvent"
1048	               (id, "organizationId", "founderId", "uploadId", action, detail, "createdAt")
1049	               VALUES (%s, %s, %s, %s, %s, %s, %s)''',
1050	            (
1051	                "ae_" + uuid.uuid4().hex[:24],
1052	                row["organizationId"],
1053	                row["founderId"],
1054	                upload_id,
1055	                "ingest",
1056	                _db_safe(audit_detail, cap=500),
1057	                now,
1058	            ),
1059	        )
1060	
1061	        conn.commit()
1062	        return IngestFromCodexResult(
1063	            upload_id=upload_id,
1064	            title=row["title"] or row["originalName"] or upload_id,
1065	            num_claims_extracted=len(claims),
1066	            num_conclusions_written=written,
1067	            num_contradictions_written=contradictions_written,

1069	            num_research_suggestions_written=research_suggestions_written,
1070	            dry_run=False,

1077	        try:
1078	            err = psycopg2.connect(url)
1079	            ec = err.cursor()
1080	            ec.execute(
1081	                '''UPDATE "Upload"
1082	                   SET status = %s, "errorMessage" = %s
1083	                   WHERE id = %s''',
1084	                (

1086	                    _db_safe(
1087	                        f"Local ingest-from-codex failed: {sys.exc_info()[1]}",
1088	                        cap=400,

1090	                    upload_id,
