"""PDF extractor: pypdf primary path, OCR fallback gate, failure cases.

Fixtures are synthesized at test time (via reportlab + Pillow) rather
than checked in — same rationale as the audio fixtures: keeps the repo
lean and sidesteps the "who owns this file?" question. Both fixtures
are cached under ``tests/fixtures/`` so repeat runs are free.
"""

from __future__ import annotations

import os
from io import BytesIO
from pathlib import Path
from unittest import mock

import pytest

from noosphere.extractors.base import BinaryContent, ExtractionFailed
from noosphere.extractors._pdf_text import PageText, digital_confidence
from noosphere.extractors.pdf_extractor import PdfExtractor


_FIXTURES_DIR = Path(__file__).parent / "fixtures"
_TINY_DIGITAL = _FIXTURES_DIR / "tiny_digital.pdf"
_TINY_SCANNED = _FIXTURES_DIR / "tiny_scanned.pdf"

# ~200 chars per page, two pages — well above the 80-char floor so
# digital_confidence comes out to 1.0 on a clean pypdf extraction.
_PAGE_BODIES = [
    (
        "Page one of the digital fixture. Noosphere ingests PDFs through a "
        "two-stage pipeline: pypdf first, OCR only on scanned images. This "
        "sentence exists to push the character count past the floor."
    ),
    (
        "Page two of the digital fixture. The [page N] marker between pages "
        "gives downstream claim extractors a citation anchor back to the "
        "original PDF so sourceRef fields can point at a specific page."
    ),
]


def _bin(data: bytes) -> BinaryContent:
    return BinaryContent(
        data=data, mime="application/pdf", filename="x.pdf", source="local"
    )


def _require(module_name: str) -> None:
    pytest.importorskip(module_name)


@pytest.fixture(scope="session")
def tiny_digital_pdf() -> Path:
    """A 2-page digital PDF with real text objects."""
    _require("reportlab")
    if _TINY_DIGITAL.exists() and _TINY_DIGITAL.stat().st_size > 0:
        return _TINY_DIGITAL

    from reportlab.lib.pagesizes import letter
    from reportlab.pdfgen import canvas

    _FIXTURES_DIR.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
    c = canvas.Canvas(str(_TINY_DIGITAL), pagesize=letter)
    width, _ = letter
    for body in _PAGE_BODIES:
        # Wrap the long line across the page at roughly 70 chars each.
        y = 720
        for chunk in _wrap(body, 70):
            c.drawString(72, y, chunk)
            y -= 18
        c.showPage()
    c.save()
    return _TINY_DIGITAL


@pytest.fixture(scope="session")
def tiny_scanned_pdf() -> Path:
    """Same content rendered as an image and embedded — pypdf recovers ~0 chars."""
    _require("reportlab")
    _require("PIL")
    if _TINY_SCANNED.exists() and _TINY_SCANNED.stat().st_size > 0:
        return _TINY_SCANNED

    from PIL import Image, ImageDraw, ImageFont
    from reportlab.lib.pagesizes import letter
    from reportlab.pdfgen import canvas
    from reportlab.lib.utils import ImageReader

    _FIXTURES_DIR.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
    page_w, page_h = letter

    c = canvas.Canvas(str(_TINY_SCANNED), pagesize=letter)
    try:
        font = ImageFont.truetype("Helvetica", 18)
    except (OSError, IOError):
        font = ImageFont.load_default()
    for body in _PAGE_BODIES:
        img = Image.new("RGB", (850, 1100), color="white")
        draw = ImageDraw.Draw(img)
        y = 80
        for chunk in _wrap(body, 70):
            draw.text((60, y), chunk, fill="black", font=font)
            y += 28
        buf = BytesIO()
        img.save(buf, format="PNG")
        buf.seek(0)
        c.drawImage(ImageReader(buf), 0, 0, width=page_w, height=page_h)
        c.showPage()
    c.save()
    return _TINY_SCANNED


def _wrap(text: str, width: int) -> list[str]:
    words = text.split()
    out: list[str] = []
    line = ""
    for w in words:
        candidate = f"{line} {w}".strip()
        if len(candidate) > width:
            if line:
                out.append(line)
            line = w
        else:
            line = candidate
    if line:
        out.append(line)
    return out


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------
# Primary path: digitally produced PDF
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------


def test_digital_pdf_uses_pypdf(tiny_digital_pdf: Path):
    _require("pypdf")
    result = PdfExtractor().extract(_bin(tiny_digital_pdf.read_bytes()))
    assert result.extraction_method == "pypdf"
    assert result.source_format == "application/pdf"
    assert "[page 1]" in result.text
    assert "[page 2]" in result.text
    # Some of the real prose from page one must survive pypdf.
    assert "two-stage pipeline" in result.text


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------
# OCR gate: scanned PDF with the env flag unset
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------


def test_scanned_pdf_without_ocr_flag_fails(tiny_scanned_pdf: Path, monkeypatch):
    _require("pypdf")
    monkeypatch.delenv("NOOSPHERE_ENABLE_OCR", raising=False)
    with pytest.raises(ExtractionFailed) as exc_info:
        PdfExtractor().extract(_bin(tiny_scanned_pdf.read_bytes()))
    msg = str(exc_info.value)
    assert "OCR is disabled" in msg
    assert "NOOSPHERE_ENABLE_OCR" in msg


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------
# OCR enabled but the binary is missing
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------


def test_scanned_pdf_ocr_enabled_but_binary_missing(
    tiny_scanned_pdf: Path, monkeypatch
):
    _require("pypdf")
    monkeypatch.setenv("NOOSPHERE_ENABLE_OCR", "1")
    with mock.patch(
        "noosphere.extractors._pdf_ocr.shutil.which", return_value=None
    ):
        with pytest.raises(ExtractionFailed) as exc_info:
            PdfExtractor().extract(_bin(tiny_scanned_pdf.read_bytes()))
    assert "install ocrmypdf" in str(exc_info.value).lower()


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------
# OCR enabled end-to-end (only runs if ocrmypdf is actually on PATH)
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------


def test_scanned_pdf_ocr(tiny_scanned_pdf: Path, monkeypatch):
    _require("pypdf")
    import shutil

    if shutil.which("ocrmypdf") is None:
        pytest.skip("ocrmypdf not on PATH; install via `brew install ocrmypdf`.")
    monkeypatch.setenv("NOOSPHERE_ENABLE_OCR", "1")
    result = PdfExtractor().extract(_bin(tiny_scanned_pdf.read_bytes()))
    assert result.extraction_method == "ocrmypdf"
    assert result.text.strip()  # tesseract recovered *something*


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------
# Corrupt input
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------


def test_corrupt_bytes_raise_extraction_failed():
    _require("pypdf")
    garbage = os.urandom(64)
    with pytest.raises(ExtractionFailed):
        PdfExtractor().extract(_bin(garbage))


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------
# Confidence heuristic — 2 of 8 pages above the floor → exactly 0.25
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------


def test_digital_confidence_at_threshold():
    pages = [
        PageText(page_number=i + 1, text="x" * (200 if i < 2 else 0),
                 char_count=(200 if i < 2 else 0))
        for i in range(8)
    ]
    assert digital_confidence(pages) == pytest.approx(0.25)


def test_digital_confidence_empty_pages_returns_zero():
    assert digital_confidence([]) == 0.0

1	"""Text extractor: UTF-8 passthrough, latin-1 fallback with warning,
2	binary-sniff refusal, and JSON pretty-print."""

4	from __future__ import annotations
5	
6	import json
7	import os
8	
9	import pytest

11	from noosphere.extractors.base import (
12	    BinaryContent,
13	    ExtractionFailed,
14	)
15	from noosphere.extractors.text_extractor import TextExtractor

18	def _bin(data: bytes, mime: str = "text/plain") -> BinaryContent:
19	    return BinaryContent(data=data, mime=mime, filename="x.txt", source="local")
20	
21	
22	def test_utf8_passthrough():
23	    result = TextExtractor().extract(_bin("Café résumé naïve\n".encode("utf-8")))
24	    assert result.text == "Café résumé naïve\n"
25	    assert result.extraction_method == "utf-8"
26	    assert result.warnings == []
27	
28	
29	def test_latin1_fallback_warns():
30	    # A byte that's invalid UTF-8 but valid latin-1 (0xE9 alone = é in latin-1,
31	    # but is a continuation byte in UTF-8 and therefore a decode error).
32	    data = b"caf\xe9 au lait"
33	    result = TextExtractor().extract(_bin(data))
34	    assert "café au lait" in result.text.lower()
35	    assert result.extraction_method == "latin-1-fallback"
36	    assert any("latin-1" in w for w in result.warnings)
37	
38	
39	def test_binary_noise_refused():
40	    # Heavy NUL content — the kind of thing os.urandom(...) occasionally
41	    # produces, but with a deterministic majority of NULs so the sniff trips
42	    # reliably on every run. Pure `os.urandom` has only ~12% NUL+control
43	    # bytes which is under the 30% threshold, so use a seeded synthesis.
44	    payload = b"\x00" * 64 + os.urandom(16)
45	    with pytest.raises(ExtractionFailed) as exc_info:
46	        TextExtractor().extract(_bin(payload))
47	    assert "do not look like text" in str(exc_info.value)
48	
49	
50	def test_json_pretty_printed():
51	    raw = json.dumps({"b": 2, "a": [1, 2, 3]}).encode("utf-8")
52	    result = TextExtractor().extract(_bin(raw, mime="application/json"))
53	    # Pretty-printed form has newlines + indentation we didn't feed in.
54	    assert "\n" in result.text
55	    assert "  " in result.text
56	    # And still parses back to equivalent data.
57	    assert json.loads(result.text) == {"b": 2, "a": [1, 2, 3]}
58	    assert "json-pretty" in result.extraction_method
59	
60	
61	def test_json_invalid_falls_back_to_raw():
62	    raw = b'{"this is not": json,'
63	    result = TextExtractor().extract(_bin(raw, mime="application/json"))
64	    assert result.text.startswith('{"this is not"')
65	    assert any("json parse failed" in w for w in result.warnings)

"""Text extractor: UTF-8 passthrough, latin-1 fallback with warning,
binary-sniff refusal, and JSON pretty-print."""

from __future__ import annotations

import json
import os

import pytest

from noosphere.extractors.base import (
    BinaryContent,
    ExtractionFailed,
)
from noosphere.extractors.text_extractor import TextExtractor


def _bin(data: bytes, mime: str = "text/plain") -> BinaryContent:
    return BinaryContent(data=data, mime=mime, filename="x.txt", source="local")


def test_utf8_passthrough():
    result = TextExtractor().extract(_bin("Café résumé naïve\n".encode("utf-8")))
    assert result.text == "Café résumé naïve\n"
    assert result.extraction_method == "utf-8"
    assert result.warnings == []


def test_latin1_fallback_warns():
    # A byte that's invalid UTF-8 but valid latin-1 (0xE9 alone = é in latin-1,
    # but is a continuation byte in UTF-8 and therefore a decode error).
    data = b"caf\xe9 au lait"
    result = TextExtractor().extract(_bin(data))
    assert "café au lait" in result.text.lower()
    assert result.extraction_method == "latin-1-fallback"
    assert any("latin-1" in w for w in result.warnings)


def test_binary_noise_refused():
    # Heavy NUL content — the kind of thing os.urandom(...) occasionally
    # produces, but with a deterministic majority of NULs so the sniff trips
    # reliably on every run. Pure `os.urandom` has only ~12% NUL+control
    # bytes which is under the 30% threshold, so use a seeded synthesis.
    payload = b"\x00" * 64 + os.urandom(16)
    with pytest.raises(ExtractionFailed) as exc_info:
        TextExtractor().extract(_bin(payload))
    assert "do not look like text" in str(exc_info.value)


def test_json_pretty_printed():
    raw = json.dumps({"b": 2, "a": [1, 2, 3]}).encode("utf-8")
    result = TextExtractor().extract(_bin(raw, mime="application/json"))
    # Pretty-printed form has newlines + indentation we didn't feed in.
    assert "\n" in result.text
    assert "  " in result.text
    # And still parses back to equivalent data.
    assert json.loads(result.text) == {"b": 2, "a": [1, 2, 3]}
    assert "json-pretty" in result.extraction_method


def test_json_invalid_falls_back_to_raw():
    raw = b'{"this is not": json,'
    result = TextExtractor().extract(_bin(raw, mime="application/json"))
    assert result.text.startswith('{"this is not"')
    assert any("json parse failed" in w for w in result.warnings)

1	generator client {
2	  provider = "prisma-client-js"
3	  // No custom `output` — Prisma 7 puts types alongside the runtime at
4	  // `node_modules/.prisma/client` automatically when you leave this blank,
5	  // and `@prisma/client` re-exports them. Explicitly setting
6	  // `output = "../node_modules/.prisma/client"` used to work on Prisma 6
7	  // but in Prisma 7 it only emits the WASM runtime (no .d.ts files),
8	  // which breaks every `import { PrismaClient } from "@prisma/client"`.
9	}
10	
11	// Postgres everywhere (Supabase / Neon / local Docker Postgres). Prisma bakes
12	// the provider into the generated migrations, so you can't switch to SQLite
13	// per-env with the same migration history. Use a local Postgres container
14	// (see `docker-compose.dev.yml`) for offline dev, or point DATABASE_URL at
15	// your Supabase project for both dev and prod.
16	datasource db {
17	  provider = "postgresql"
18	}
19	
20	// ── Multi-tenant root (cloud: Postgres + RLS; local dev: SQLite) ─────────────
21	
22	model Organization {
23	  id        String    @id @default(cuid())
24	  /// URL-safe tenant key (login, subdomains, worker routing)
25	  slug      String    @unique
26	  name      String
27	  /// Soft-delete grace window (hard-delete after policy window)
28	  deletedAt DateTime?
29	  createdAt DateTime  @default(now())
30	  updatedAt DateTime  @updatedAt
31	
32	  founders             Founder[]
33	  sessions             Session[]
34	  uploads              Upload[]
35	  conclusions          Conclusion[]
36	  contradictions       Contradiction[]
37	  driftEvents          DriftEvent[]
38	  researchSuggestions  ResearchSuggestion[]
39	  reviewItems          ReviewItem[]
40	  openQuestions        OpenQuestion[]
41	  auditEvents          AuditEvent[]
42	  publicationReviews   PublicationReview[]
43	  publishedConclusions PublishedConclusion[]
44	  publicResponses      PublicResponse[]
45	  apiKeys              ApiKey[]
46	}
47	
48	// ── Founders (portal users) — maps to FounderProfile in noosphere/models.py ─
49	
50	model Founder {
51	  id             String       @id @default(cuid())
52	  organizationId String
53	  organization   Organization @relation(fields: [organizationId], references: [id], onDelete: Restrict)
54	
55	  name         String
56	  username     String
57	  email        String
58	  passwordHash String
59	  // Role ladder — see src/lib/roles.ts for the predicate helpers and
60	  // the full description of each tier:
61	  //   admin   → full read + write + can change other founders' roles
62	  //   founder → full read + write
63	  //   viewer  → read-only; every write API returns 403 and every UI
64	  //              affordance for writes is hidden
65	  // Default "founder" preserves prior behaviour: anyone added via
66	  // scripts/add-founder.ts is read+write unless explicitly seeded
67	  // with ADD_FOUNDER_ROLE=viewer or =admin. Validation lives at the
68	  // application layer; the column stays TEXT so adding a future tier
69	  // is a docstring + helper change, not a migration.
70	  role         String  @default("founder") // admin | founder | viewer
71	  bio          String?
72	  avatar       String?
73	
74	  /// Maps to FounderProfile.id in Noosphere
75	  noosphereId String?
76	
77	  createdAt DateTime @default(now())
78	  updatedAt DateTime @updatedAt
79	
80	  sessions                   Session[]
81	  uploads                    Upload[]
82	  auditEvents                AuditEvent[]
83	  deletionRequests           DeletionRequest[]
84	  attributedConclusions      Conclusion[]                @relation("ConclusionFounder")
85	  researchSuggestions        ResearchSuggestion[]
86	  reviewResolutions          ReviewItem[]                @relation("ReviewResolver")
87	  publicationReviews         PublicationReview[]         @relation("PublicationReviewer")
88	  apiKeys                    ApiKey[]
89	  conclusionDeletionRequests ConclusionDeletionRequest[]
90	  dashboardDismissals        DashboardDismissal[]
91	  resolvedContradictions     Contradiction[]             @relation("ContradictionResolver")
92	
93	  @@unique([organizationId, email])
94	  @@unique([organizationId, username])
95	  @@index([organizationId])
96	}
97	
98	/// Browser session (server-side row; cookie carries signed opaque token)
99	model Session {
100	  id             String   @id @default(cuid())
101	  organizationId String
102	  founderId      String
103	  token          String   @unique
104	  expiresAt      DateTime
105	  createdAt      DateTime @default(now())
106	
107	  founder      Founder      @relation(fields: [founderId], references: [id], onDelete: Cascade)
108	  organization Organization @relation(fields: [organizationId], references: [id], onDelete: Restrict)
109	
110	  @@index([organizationId])
111	  @@index([founderId])
112	}
113	
114	// ── Uploads ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
115	
116	model Upload {
117	  id             String @id @default(cuid())
118	  organizationId String
119	  founderId      String
120	
121	  title            String
122	  description      String?
123	  sourceType       String   @default("written") // written | annotation | external | transcript
124	  originalName     String
125	  mimeType         String
126	  filePath         String
127	  fileSize         Int
128	  textContent      String?
129	  // Status ladder (see src/lib/uploadStatus.ts for the canonical
130	  // vocabulary + UI labels). `pending | processing | ingested | failed`
131	  // are load-bearing strings from the init migration; `extracting` and
132	  // `awaiting_ingest` were added alongside the Wave-1 audio/PDF
133	  // extractors so founders can see which stage of the pipeline a row is
134	  // in (Whisper/pypdf vs. claim extraction) instead of a single opaque
135	  // "processing".
136	  status           String   @default("pending") // pending | extracting | awaiting_ingest | processing | ingested | failed
137	  processLog       String   @default("") // streamed CLI stdout/stderr
138	  claimsCount      Int?
139	  methodCount      Int?
140	  substCount       Int?
141	  principleCount   Int?
142	  errorMessage     String?
143	  // How the text was obtained for this upload, set by the noosphere
144	  // extractor dispatcher on success. Free-form string — e.g.
145	  // "passthrough", "faster-whisper", "openai-whisper-1", "pypdf",
146	  // "ocrmypdf". Rendered in the dashboard detail row so the founder
147	  // knows whether a transcript/PDF extract was reliable.
148	  extractionMethod String?
149	  createdAt        DateTime @default(now())
150	  updatedAt        DateTime @updatedAt
151	
152	  // ── Public blog fields ─────────────────────────────────────────────
153	  // An Upload becomes a public blog post when `publishedAt IS NOT NULL`.
154	  // Default is null → the upload remains a private founder artifact.
155	  // `slug` is the URL-safe identifier used for `/post/:slug`; it's
156	  // generated from `title` at publish time and guaranteed unique across
157	  // the whole table (not per-org — the blog is a single public surface).
158	  // `blogExcerpt` is the card/list summary shown on the index; if null
159	  // we derive one from the first ~280 chars of `textContent`.
160	  // `authorBio` lets the founder override the displayed byline (e.g.
161	  // for guest posts or co-authored work); otherwise we show
162	  // Founder.name.
163	  publishedAt DateTime?
164	  slug        String?   @unique
165	  blogExcerpt String?
166	  authorBio   String?
167	
168	  // ── Soft delete ────────────────────────────────────────────────────
169	  // `deletedAt` is the single source of truth for "this upload has
170	  // been removed". Every query that surfaces uploads to the UI must
171	  // filter by `deletedAt IS NULL`; we keep the row around so the audit
172	  // trail (AuditEvent pointing at this upload) stays intact, and so a
173	  // DB-level restore is possible if a deletion turns out to be a
174	  // mistake. Only the uploader can set this directly; peers must go
175	  // through a DeletionRequest, which the owner accepts (setting
176	  // `deletedAt=now`) or declines.
177	  deletedAt DateTime?
178	
179	  // ── Visibility inside the firm ─────────────────────────────────────
180	  // Three-level visibility ladder. Every value still lets Noosphere
181	  // run ingestion — conclusions, contradictions, open questions, and
182	  // research suggestions are always extracted. The levels only
183	  // control *who reads the upload's metadata and contents*.
184	  //
185	  // "org"          → visible to every founder in the organization AND
186	  //                   eligible for blog publication. The default;
187	  //                   library, dashboard, peer deletion requests, and
188	  //                   the blog's publishAsPost toggle all apply.
189	  //
190	  // "semi-private" → visible to every founder in the organization,
191	  //                   but never publishable to the public blog. The
192	  //                   intermediate level, for material the firm wants
193	  //                   to discuss internally but that must never leak
194	  //                   outside. /api/publish rejects the flip; the
195	  //                   public blog index + /post/[slug] both filter
196	  //                   it out; /library and /dashboard render it with
197	  //                   a "semi-private" badge so peers know the
198	  //                   constraint.
199	  //
200	  // "private"      → only the uploader can see the row anywhere in
201	  //                   the UI; Noosphere still ingests it so the owner
202	  //                   gets their own conclusions/contradictions, but
203	  //                   nobody else can see the file, read it, or open
204	  //                   a deletion request against it. Blog-publishing
205	  //                   a private upload is rejected at the API layer
206	  //                   — flip visibility first.
207	  //
208	  // Column stays TEXT with default "org" so no migration is needed
209	  // when new levels are introduced; validation happens at the API
210	  // layer.
211	  visibility String @default("org")
212	
213	  // ── Podcast / audio playback ───────────────────────────────────────
214	  // When an audio upload is stored in a public CDN-addressable
215	  // location (Supabase Storage in our case), we persist the public
216	  // URL here so /post/[slug] can render a <audio controls> player
217	  // alongside the transcript. `audioDurationSec` is optional
218	  // metadata derived from ffprobe / client-side duration detection
219	  // when available; the UI falls back to the player's own duration
220	  // readout if it's null. Both fields are null for non-audio rows
221	  // and for audio rows uploaded before Supabase Storage was
222	  // configured — the blog post still renders, just text-only.
223	  audioUrl         String?
224	  audioDurationSec Int?
225	
226	  founder             Founder              @relation(fields: [founderId], references: [id])
227	  organization        Organization         @relation(fields: [organizationId], references: [id], onDelete: Restrict)
228	  auditEvents         AuditEvent[]
229	  deletionRequests    DeletionRequest[]
230	  // ── Provenance back-refs ─────────────────────────────────────────
231	  // Derived entities (Conclusions via the M:N join, Contradictions,
232	  // OpenQuestions, ResearchSuggestions directly) point back here so
233	  // the delete path can cascade-clean any artifact whose source has
234	  // been removed. `conclusionSources` is the many-to-many bridge —
235	  // the same text can be extracted from multiple uploads, and each
236	  // upload holds its own row in the bridge. The direct sourceUpload
237	  // relations on the other three entities are one-per-row: a
238	  // contradiction / open-question / research suggestion is an output
239	  // of exactly one ingestion run, even when it references claims from
240	  // multiple uploads.
241	  conclusionSources   ConclusionSource[]
242	  contradictions      Contradiction[]      @relation("ContradictionSourceUpload")
243	  openQuestions       OpenQuestion[]       @relation("OpenQuestionSourceUpload")
244	  researchSuggestions ResearchSuggestion[] @relation("ResearchSuggestionSourceUpload")
245	
246	  @@index([organizationId])
247	  @@index([founderId])
248	  @@index([publishedAt])
249	  @@index([deletedAt])
250	  @@index([visibility])
251	  // Dashboard filters by status constantly (ingested rows on the main
252	  // panel, failed/pending counts on the banner). A plain btree index
253	  // is enough — the org-scope is already covered by organizationId.
254	  @@index([status])
255	}
256	
257	// ── DeletionRequest ───────────────────────────────────────────────────
258	// Peer-to-peer "please delete this" workflow. A founder who doesn't own
259	// an upload but wants it removed opens a request against that upload;
260	// the owner reviews it (accept → soft-delete the upload, decline → log
261	// reason and keep). The requester can cancel their own request at any
262	// time before the owner responds.
263	//
264	// Enforcement:
265	//   * Requester.organizationId must equal Upload.organizationId (done
266	//     at route-handler level, not schema — cross-org requests are
267	//     structurally unreachable via UI anyway).
268	//   * Requester.id MUST NOT equal Upload.founderId (an owner deletes
269	//     their own upload directly via DELETE /api/uploads/:id; opening
270	//     a request against yourself is noise).
271	//   * At most one ACTIVE (status=pending) request per (upload, requester)
272	//     pair — the unique partial-index is added in the migration SQL.
273	
274	model DeletionRequest {
275	  id          String    @id @default(cuid())
276	  uploadId    String
277	  requesterId String
278	  status      String    @default("pending") // pending | accepted | declined | cancelled
279	  reason      String?
280	  decision    String? // optional note from the owner when accepting/declining
281	  createdAt   DateTime  @default(now())
282	  respondedAt DateTime?
283	
284	  upload    Upload  @relation(fields: [uploadId], references: [id], onDelete: Cascade)
285	  requester Founder @relation(fields: [requesterId], references: [id])
286	
287	  @@index([uploadId])
288	  @@index([requesterId])
289	  @@index([status])
290	}
291	
292	// ── Conclusion — mirrors noosphere.models.Conclusion ───────────────────────
293	
294	model Conclusion {
295	  id             String @id @default(cuid())
296	  organizationId String
297	
298	  /// Stable id if synced from / produced for Noosphere
299	  noosphereId String? @unique
300	  text        String
301	
302	  // ── Dedup key ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────
303	  // `normalizedText` is `LOWER(TRIM(REGEXP_REPLACE(text, '\s+', ' ')))`
304	  // computed once at ingest time. The ingest pipeline checks for an
305	  // existing Conclusion in the same org with a matching normalizedText;
306	  // if one exists, it adds the current upload as an ADDITIONAL source
307	  // via ConclusionSource rather than inserting a duplicate row. That's
308	  // what makes deletion correct: when two uploads produced the same
309	  // claim and we delete one, the conclusion stays — it's still
310	  // supported by the other source.
311	  //
312	  // Nullable so the column can be added to an existing DB without a
313	  // rewrite; the migration back-fills every existing row. Indexed
314	  // `(organizationId, normalizedText)` because every lookup is scoped
315	  // to a single org.
316	  normalizedText         String?
317	  confidenceTier         String // firm | founder | open (ConfidenceTier)
318	  rationale              String   @default("")
319	  supportingPrincipleIds String   @default("[]") // JSON string[]
320	  evidenceChainClaimIds  String   @default("[]")
321	  dissentClaimIds        String   @default("[]")
322	  confidence             Float    @default(0)
323	  topicHint              String   @default("") // UI filter / display
324	  createdAt              DateTime @default(now())
325	
326	  /// JSON-serialized float[] embedding produced by Noosphere's sentence
327	  /// encoder at ingest time. Null for conclusions ingested before this
328	  /// column was added; the semantic explorer backfills on demand.
329	  embeddingJson String?
330	
331	  attributedFounderId String?
332	  attributedFounder   Founder?                    @relation("ConclusionFounder", fields: [attributedFounderId], references: [id])
333	  organization        Organization                @relation(fields: [organizationId], references: [id], onDelete: Restrict)
334	  publicationReviews  PublicationReview[]
335	  sources             ConclusionSource[]
336	  deletionRequests    ConclusionDeletionRequest[]
337	  dashboardDismissals DashboardDismissal[]
338	
339	  @@index([organizationId])
340	  @@index([organizationId, normalizedText])
341	}
342	
343	/// Many-to-many bridge Conclusion ↔ Upload.
344	///
345	/// Every conclusion that came out of the ingest pipeline has at least
346	/// one row here tying it back to the upload it was extracted from.
347	/// When the SAME normalized text appears in multiple uploads, the
348	/// ingest pipeline reuses the existing Conclusion row and adds a new
349	/// ConclusionSource row per additional upload, so the upstream count
350	/// can drop from 2 → 1 → 0 as uploads are deleted.
351	///
352	/// Delete semantics (enforced by the app-side delete handlers, NOT by
353	/// FK cascade — we soft-delete uploads, so the FK's ON DELETE CASCADE
354	/// would not fire anyway):
355	///
356	///   * When an upload is soft-deleted, every ConclusionSource row
357	///     with that uploadId is hard-deleted.
358	///   * After that, any Conclusion with zero remaining rows is
359	///     hard-deleted too (the claim's last source is gone).
360	///   * Conclusions with sources from other uploads remain, just
361	///     with one fewer row.
362	///
363	/// The FK cascade below still serves as a safety net for the rare
364	/// case of a hard-delete (org takedown, privacy-law purge).
365	model ConclusionSource {
366	  conclusionId String
367	  uploadId     String
368	  createdAt    DateTime @default(now())
369	
370	  conclusion Conclusion @relation(fields: [conclusionId], references: [id], onDelete: Cascade)
371	  upload     Upload     @relation(fields: [uploadId], references: [id], onDelete: Cascade)
372	
373	  @@id([conclusionId, uploadId])
374	  @@index([conclusionId])
375	  @@index([uploadId])
376	}
377

393

396	  updatedAt         DateTime @updatedAt
397	
398	  organization Organization @relation(fields: [organizationId], references: [id], onDelete: Restrict)
399	  target       Conclusion   @relation(fields: [conclusionId], references: [id], onDelete: Cascade)
400	  reviewer     Founder?     @relation("PublicationReviewer", fields: [reviewerFounderId], references: [id])

460	  published    PublishedConclusion @relation(fields: [publishedConclusionId], references: [id], onDelete: Cascade)

467	// ── ContradictionFinding + scores — mirrors pipeline outputs ────────────────
468	
469	model Contradiction {
470	  id             String @id @default(cuid())
471	  organizationId String
472	
473	  claimAId     String
474	  claimBId     String
475	  severity     Float
476	  sixLayerJson String?
477	  narrative    String   @default("")
478	  createdAt    DateTime @default(now())
479	
480	  // ── Source tracking ───────────────────────────────────────────────
481	  // The upload whose ingestion run produced this contradiction row.
482	  // Nullable so the column can be backfilled from `noosphereId` patterns
483	  // on existing rows; the ingest pipeline sets it on every new row.
484	  // When the source upload is deleted the contradiction is hard-deleted
485	  // in the same transaction (see src/app/api/upload/[id]/delete/route.ts);
486	  // cross-upload contradictions (where claimA and claimB come from
487	  // different uploads) additionally die if EITHER of their referenced
488	  // Conclusions has already been orphan-deleted.
489	  sourceUploadId String?
490	  sourceUpload   Upload? @relation("ContradictionSourceUpload", fields: [sourceUploadId], references: [id])
491	
492	  // ── Resolution tracking ────────────────────────────────────────────
493	  // A contradiction starts "active". A founder can resolve it
494	  // (acknowledged + addressed with a note) or dismiss it (false
495	  // positive). Non-active rows are hidden from the main view by
496	  // default but preserved for audit.
497	  status       String    @default("active") // active | resolved | dismissed
498	  resolution   String?
499	  resolvedById String?
500	  resolvedAt   DateTime?
501	  resolvedBy   Founder?  @relation("ContradictionResolver", fields: [resolvedById], references: [id])
502	
503	  organization Organization @relation(fields: [organizationId], references: [id], onDelete: Restrict)
504	
505	  @@index([organizationId])
506	  @@index([sourceUploadId])
507	  @@index([status])
508	}

510	// ── ConclusionDeletionRequest ─────────────────────────────────────────
511	// Peer-to-peer "please delete this conclusion" workflow, mirroring
512	// DeletionRequest for uploads. A founder opens a request; the
513	// conclusion's attributed founder (or an admin) accepts or declines.

515	// via FK cascade) so it's purged from the model.

523	  decision     String?

526	
527	  conclusion Conclusion @relation(fields: [conclusionId], references: [id], onDelete: Cascade)

529	
530	  @@index([conclusionId])
531	  @@index([requesterId])
532	  @@index([status])
533	}

681	// ── API keys for machine-to-machine auth (Dialectic auto-sync, scripts, etc.) ─
682	//
683	// Store the *hash* of the key, never the plaintext. The plaintext is returned
684	// once at creation time. Revocation = set `revokedAt`. The key plaintext
685	// should look like `tcx_<founderId>_<64-hex-random>` so the client can send
686	// the founderId bits without a DB lookup for routing, and the random tail is
687	// the secret. `authenticateApiKey()` in src/lib/apiKeyAuth.ts implements
688	// the verification (bcrypt compare against `keyHash`).
689	model ApiKey {
690	  id             String @id @default(cuid())
691	  organizationId String
692	  /// Founder the key acts on behalf of — uploads are attributed to them.
693	  founderId      String
694	
695	  /// Human label shown in the management UI (e.g. "Dialectic on laptop").
696	  label   String
697	  /// Short prefix of the plaintext (first 12 chars) — shown in UI for identification.
698	  prefix  String
699	  /// bcrypt hash of the full plaintext key. Never store plaintext.
700	  keyHash String
701	  /// Comma-separated scopes (e.g. "upload:write"). Empty = full founder scope.
702	  scopes  String @default("")
703	
704	  createdAt  DateTime  @default(now())
705	  lastUsedAt DateTime?
706	  revokedAt  DateTime?
707	
708	  founder      Founder      @relation(fields: [founderId], references: [id], onDelete: Cascade)
709	  organization Organization @relation(fields: [organizationId], references: [id], onDelete: Restrict)
710	
711	  @@index([organizationId])
712	  @@index([founderId])
713	  @@index([prefix])
714	}
715

[project]
name = "noosphere"
version = "0.1.0"
description = "The Brain of the Firm — epistemological engine for Theseus"
requires-python = ">=3.11"
license = {text = "Proprietary"}

[project.scripts]
noosphere = "noosphere.__main__:main"

[project.optional-dependencies]
# Local transcription via faster-whisper (CTranslate2 backend). Heavy:
# pulls in ctranslate2 + onnxruntime wheels (~150 MB). Dialectic already
# ships the same stack, so on a monorepo checkout this is a no-op. Keep
# optional so text/PDF-only ingest stays lean.
audio = [
    "faster-whisper >= 1.0, < 2.0",
    "mutagen >= 1.47",
]
# Cloud Whisper fallback (OpenAI). Imported lazily — only required when
# faster-whisper is unavailable or NOOSPHERE_FORCE_OPENAI_WHISPER=1.
whisper-openai = [
    "openai >= 1.30",
]
# PDF text extraction (pypdf). OCR fallback for scanned PDFs uses
# `ocrmypdf` from PATH (system-installed via brew/apt) and is gated on
# NOOSPHERE_ENABLE_OCR=1 — not a pip-installable extra.
pdf = [
    "pypdf >= 5.0",
]
# Test-only dependencies for synthesizing PDF fixtures at test time.
dev = [
    "reportlab >= 4.0",
    "Pillow >= 10.0",
]

[build-system]
requires = ["setuptools>=68.0"]
build-backend = "setuptools.build_meta"

[tool.setuptools.packages.find]
include = ["noosphere*"]
exclude = ["tests*", "scripts*", "alembic*"]

1	"""Text-family extractor: passthrough for ``text/*``, pretty-printing
2	for JSON, and a latin-1 fallback for text files that aren't valid UTF-8."""
3	
4	from __future__ import annotations
5	
6	import json
7	
8	from noosphere.extractors.base import (
9	    BinaryContent,
10	    ExtractedText,
11	    ExtractionFailed,
12	)
13	
14	
15	_TEXT_PREFIXES = ("text/",)
16	_TEXT_APPLICATION_TYPES = (
17	    "application/json",
18	    "application/ld+json",
19	    "application/xml",
20	    "application/x-ndjson",
21	)
22	
23	
24	class TextExtractor:
25	    name = "text"
26	    mime_prefixes = _TEXT_PREFIXES + _TEXT_APPLICATION_TYPES
27	
28	    def extract(self, content: BinaryContent) -> ExtractedText:
29	        warnings: list[str] = []
30	        text, method = _decode(content.data, warnings)
31	
32	        if _is_json_mime(content.mime):
33	            try:
34	                parsed = json.loads(text)
35	                text = json.dumps(parsed, indent=2, ensure_ascii=False)
36	                method = f"{method}+json-pretty"
37	            except json.JSONDecodeError as exc:
38	                warnings.append(f"json parse failed ({exc.msg}); returning raw text")
39	
40	        return ExtractedText(
41	            text=text,
42	            source_format=content.mime,
43	            extraction_method=method,
44	            warnings=warnings,
45	        )
46	
47	
48	def _is_json_mime(mime: str) -> bool:
49	    return mime == "application/json" or mime.endswith("+json")
50	
51	
52	def _decode(data: bytes, warnings: list[str]) -> tuple[str, str]:
53	    try:
54	        return data.decode("utf-8"), "utf-8"
55	    except UnicodeDecodeError:
56	        pass
57	    # latin-1 is total (any byte maps to some codepoint), so `.decode("latin-1")`
58	    # never raises. That would let us "succeed" on random binary and hand the
59	    # founder a page of mojibake. Guard by sniffing: if the payload has a lot
60	    # of NUL / C0-control bytes that don't appear in real text (tab/LF/CR
61	    # excluded) it's almost certainly not text — refuse instead of passing
62	    # rubbish downstream.
63	    if _looks_binary(data):
64	        raise ExtractionFailed(
65	            "bytes do not look like text (high ratio of NUL/control bytes); "
66	            "route this MIME to a dedicated binary extractor instead."
67	        )
68	    text = data.decode("latin-1")
69	    warnings.append("decoded as latin-1 after UTF-8 failed")
70	    return text, "latin-1-fallback"
71	
72	
73	def _looks_binary(data: bytes, threshold: float = 0.30) -> bool:
74	    if not data:
75	        return False
76	    # Count bytes that would be highly unusual in text: NUL and C0 controls
77	    # that aren't TAB/LF/CR. DEL (0x7F) counts too.
78	    bad = 0
79	    for b in data:
80	        if b == 0 or (b < 0x20 and b not in (0x09, 0x0A, 0x0D)) or b == 0x7F:
81	            bad += 1
82	    return bad / len(data) >= threshold
83

-- Minimal Codex schema for SQLite-backed ingest tests.
--
-- Mirrors the subset of columns in theseus-codex/prisma/schema.prisma that
-- the ingest pipeline (noosphere/codex_bridge.py) actually reads or writes.
-- Quoted camelCase identifiers match Prisma's generated column names so the
-- pipeline SQL runs verbatim under both Postgres (prod) and SQLite (tests).
--
-- Kept deliberately lean: no multi-tenant RLS, no soft-delete fields that
-- the ingest path does not touch, no triggers beyond the status-transition
-- log used by tests. If the prod schema adds a column that ingest reads,
-- mirror it here too — otherwise the Postgres pipeline will succeed while
-- the SQLite test suite silently passes with stale fields.

CREATE TABLE "Organization" (
  "id" TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
  "slug" TEXT UNIQUE NOT NULL,
  "name" TEXT NOT NULL
);

CREATE TABLE "Upload" (
  "id" TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
  "organizationId" TEXT NOT NULL,
  "founderId" TEXT NOT NULL,
  "title" TEXT NOT NULL,
  "description" TEXT,
  "sourceType" TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'written',
  "originalName" TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT '',
  "mimeType" TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT '',
  "filePath" TEXT,
  "fileSize" INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
  "textContent" TEXT,
  "status" TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'pending',
  "processLog" TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT '',
  "claimsCount" INTEGER,
  "errorMessage" TEXT,
  "extractionMethod" TEXT,
  "createdAt" TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,
  "updatedAt" TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP
);

CREATE TABLE "Conclusion" (
  "id" TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
  "organizationId" TEXT NOT NULL,
  "noosphereId" TEXT UNIQUE,
  "text" TEXT NOT NULL,
  "normalizedText" TEXT,
  "confidenceTier" TEXT NOT NULL,
  "rationale" TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT '',
  "supportingPrincipleIds" TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT '[]',
  "evidenceChainClaimIds" TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT '[]',
  "dissentClaimIds" TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT '[]',
  "confidence" REAL NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
  "topicHint" TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT '',
  "createdAt" TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP
);

CREATE INDEX "Conclusion_org_norm_idx"
  ON "Conclusion" ("organizationId", "normalizedText");

CREATE TABLE "ConclusionSource" (
  "conclusionId" TEXT NOT NULL,
  "uploadId" TEXT NOT NULL,
  "createdAt" TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,
  PRIMARY KEY ("conclusionId", "uploadId")
);

CREATE TABLE "Contradiction" (
  "id" TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
  "organizationId" TEXT NOT NULL,
  "claimAId" TEXT NOT NULL,
  "claimBId" TEXT NOT NULL,
  "severity" REAL NOT NULL,
  "sixLayerJson" TEXT,
  "narrative" TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT '',
  "sourceUploadId" TEXT,
  "status" TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'active',
  "createdAt" TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP
);

CREATE TABLE "OpenQuestion" (
  "id" TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
  "organizationId" TEXT NOT NULL,
  "noosphereId" TEXT UNIQUE,
  "summary" TEXT NOT NULL,
  "claimAId" TEXT NOT NULL,
  "claimBId" TEXT NOT NULL,
  "unresolvedReason" TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT '',
  "layerDisagreementSummary" TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT '',
  "sourceUploadId" TEXT,
  "createdAt" TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP
);

CREATE TABLE "ResearchSuggestion" (
  "id" TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
  "organizationId" TEXT NOT NULL,
  "noosphereId" TEXT UNIQUE,
  "title" TEXT NOT NULL,
  "summary" TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT '',
  "rationale" TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT '',
  "readingUris" TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT '[]',
  "sessionLabel" TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT '',
  "suggestedForFounderId" TEXT,
  "sourceUploadId" TEXT,
  "createdAt" TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP
);

CREATE TABLE "AuditEvent" (
  "id" TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
  "organizationId" TEXT NOT NULL,
  "founderId" TEXT NOT NULL,
  "uploadId" TEXT,
  "action" TEXT NOT NULL,
  "detail" TEXT,
  "createdAt" TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP
);

-- Status-transition log. Populated by the trigger below so the ingest tests
-- can assert the ordered sequence (pending → extracting → awaiting_ingest →
-- processing → ingested) without instrumenting the pipeline itself.
CREATE TABLE "UploadStatusHistory" (
  "id" INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,
  "upload_id" TEXT NOT NULL,
  "status" TEXT NOT NULL,
  "ts" TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP
);

CREATE TRIGGER "upload_status_log"
AFTER UPDATE OF "status" ON "Upload"
FOR EACH ROW
BEGIN
  INSERT INTO "UploadStatusHistory" ("upload_id", "status")
  VALUES (NEW."id", NEW."status");
END;
